chapter_summary: Full of the Holy Spirit, Jesus is tested three times by the devil in the wilderness and defeats every temptation with Scripture. He then returns to Galilee, reads Isaiah 61 in His hometown synagogue and declares it fulfilled, drives out a demon in Capernaum, heals Peter's mother-in-law, and heals crowds at sunset before withdrawing to pray and then moving on to preach in other cities.
main_lesson: Jesus faced the devil's tests by knowing and trusting God's Word — and He won every time. We can face hard things the same way.

memory_verse.kjv: And Jesus answered him, saying, It is written, That man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.
memory_verse.little_bible: When the devil tried to trick Jesus, Jesus answered with God's Word: 'People don't live by food alone — they live by every word that comes from God!' Jesus knew His Bible and used it.

parent_guide: The temptation is the great pre-ministry showdown. Jesus defeats every temptation with 'It is written...' — the model for spiritual warfare. The Isaiah 61 reading in Nazareth is Jesus' mission statement. For children, emphasise: Jesus had the same hard feelings we do (hunger, tiredness, temptation) and overcame them. v18-19 is a beautiful passage to memorise together.
application_for_children: When something is hard or scary, we can do what Jesus did — remember and say out loud what God's Word says. The Bible is our sword against fear and temptation.

verses.kjv: And Jesus being full of the Holy Ghost returned from Jordan, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness,
verses.little_bible: After His baptism, Jesus was completely filled with the Holy Spirit. The Spirit led Him away from the Jordan River and out into the wilderness — a quiet, lonely, wild place.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Full of God's Spirit, Jesus walked into the wilderness.
verses.meaning: The Holy Spirit did not lead Jesus away from danger — He led Him straight into it. Being full of the Spirit does not mean we avoid hard times; it means we face them with God.
verses.prayer: Holy Spirit, lead me today. I want to go where You lead, even when it feels hard.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the Holy Spirit led Jesus into a difficult place instead of an easy one?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes God leads us through hard things rather than around them. Can you think of a time God led your family through something difficult?
verses.do_it_today: When something feels hard today, say: 'Holy Spirit, lead me through this.'
verses.action_challenge: Draw Jesus walking into the wilderness. Add the Holy Spirit as a gentle light guiding His steps.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus walking alone into a vast, rocky wilderness under a wide sky, a soft glow of the Spirit visible around Him. Warm and serene. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: Being forty days tempted of the devil. And in those days he did eat nothing: and when they were ended, he afterward hungered.
verses.little_bible: For forty whole days, the devil kept trying to tempt Jesus. Jesus ate nothing the whole time — no food at all for forty days! By the end, Jesus was very, very hungry.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: For forty days the devil tried to trick Jesus. Jesus was very hungry.
verses.meaning: Forty days of fasting and testing. Jesus faced the full force of temptation in a weakened physical state. He knows what it feels like to be hungry, tired, and pressed hard by the enemy.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You know what it feels like to be hungry and tired. You understand me.
verses.discussion_question: How do you feel when you are very hungry or very tired? Does it make it harder to make good choices?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus was tempted when He was at His most physically weak. The enemy often attacks us when we are tired, hungry, or discouraged. How can we prepare for those moments?
verses.do_it_today: Next time you feel tired or hungry, remember Jesus faced that too — and ask Him to help you.
verses.action_challenge: Count to forty slowly. Think about one day without food for each number. How would you feel?
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus sitting on a rocky outcrop in the desert, thin and weary after forty days, eyes still steady and calm. The vast empty wilderness surrounds Him. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And the devil said unto him, If thou be the Son of God, command this stone that it be made bread.
verses.little_bible: The devil came to Jesus and said: 'If You really are the Son of God, just tell this rock to turn into bread!' He was trying to get Jesus to use His power selfishly — to prove Himself and satisfy His hunger.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The devil said: 'If You are God's Son, turn this rock into bread!'
verses.meaning: The devil's first trick: doubt your identity and use your power for yourself. 'If you are...' is a challenge to Jesus's identity. Temptation often starts with a question about who we are.
verses.memory_phrase: The devil questions who we are.
verses.prayer: God, help me remember who I am — Your child. I don't need to prove it.
verses.discussion_question: Why was it wrong for Jesus to turn the stone into bread, even though He could have?
verses.family_discussion: The devil tried to make Jesus doubt His identity and act out of selfishness. How does knowing who we are in God protect us from temptation?
verses.do_it_today: Say out loud: 'I am a child of God. I don't need to prove anything.' Say it with confidence.
verses.action_challenge: Pick up a stone and hold it. Remember this story. Stones become bread only when God says so.
verses.illustration_prompt: A stone lying in the desert sand, the devil's shadowy presence nearby, and Jesus standing firm, eyes steady. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And Jesus answered him, saying, It is written, That man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered the devil with God's Word: 'It is written — people don't live by food alone. They need every word that comes from God!' Jesus didn't argue or panic. He used the Bible like a sword.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'People need more than food — they need God's Word!'
verses.meaning: Jesus quotes Deuteronomy 8:3. The answer to the devil is always Scripture. God's Word sustains us more deeply than physical food ever can.
verses.prayer: God, help me love Your Word as much as I love food. Feed my heart with the Bible today.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think it means to live by God's Word? How does the Bible feed us?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus used Scripture — not feelings, not argument — to answer the devil. How well does your family know God's Word well enough to use it when temptation comes?
verses.do_it_today: Read one verse from the Bible today as if it is your most important meal.
verses.action_challenge: Write or say Luke 4:4 three times until you start to remember it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking with authority, a glowing scroll or open Bible in the background, the devil stepping back. Light radiating from the words. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And the devil, taking him up into an high mountain, shewed unto him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time.
verses.little_bible: The devil took Jesus up to a very high place and showed Him every kingdom in the world — all at once, in a single flash. Every palace, every city, every land under the sky.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The devil showed Jesus every kingdom in the world all at once.
verses.meaning: This is a vision of all earthly power and glory — everything the world considers worth having. The devil is offering what Jesus came to win through the cross, but on different terms.
verses.memory_phrase: The world's glory is not worth it.
verses.prayer: God, help me see that Your way is better than the world's shortcuts.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the devil showed Jesus all the kingdoms at once? What was he trying to do?
verses.family_discussion: The devil offers what looks like everything. What does our culture offer as 'everything'? How do we help our children recognise those offers?
verses.do_it_today: Think of something the world says will make you happy. Ask God if He has something better.
verses.action_challenge: Find a map or globe. Look at all the countries. Remember: Jesus already owns everything — He made it all.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus and the devil on a high mountain peak, the entire world spread out below in a glittering panorama. The devil's figure is shadowy. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And the devil said unto him, All this power will I give thee, and the glory of them: for that is delivered unto me; and to whomsoever I will I give it.
verses.little_bible: The devil boasted: 'All this power and glory is mine to give — and I'll give it all to You!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The devil said: 'All this power is mine. I will give it to You!'
verses.meaning: The devil claims authority over the world's kingdoms. While this is a partial truth (sin gave him influence), it is also a lie — ultimate authority belongs to God. The offer is real enough to be a genuine temptation.
verses.prayer: God, You are the real King. Help me not be tricked by the devil's false promises.
verses.discussion_question: Do you think the devil was telling the whole truth? What part was he leaving out?
verses.family_discussion: The devil often mixes truth with lies. How do we teach children to spot when something sounds good but isn't the full picture?
verses.do_it_today: When someone offers you a shortcut today, stop and ask: 'Would God be pleased with this?'
verses.action_challenge: List three things people chase to feel powerful or important. Then list three things God says make us truly great.
verses.illustration_prompt: The devil gesturing broadly over a glittering city below, face confident and tempting. Jesus stands still, unimpressed. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: If thou therefore wilt worship me, all shall be thine.
verses.little_bible: The devil made his demand: 'Just bow down and worship me — and everything you see will be Yours.' It was a terrible swap: worship the enemy to gain the world.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The devil said: 'Bow down to me and it is all Yours!'
verses.meaning: The second temptation is worship — trading adoration of God for a shortcut to power. It is the offer of everything for the price of your soul.
verses.prayer: God, I worship only You. Nothing in the world is worth giving You up.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think 'worship' really means? Can we worship things other than God without realising it?
verses.family_discussion: Worship is whatever we give our greatest devotion to. What do our schedules, money, and attention reveal about what our family worships?
verses.do_it_today: Choose to worship God today — sing a song, say a thank-you prayer, or just sit quietly and tell Him He is great.
verses.action_challenge: Draw or write: 'I worship God — not money, not fame, not anything else.'
verses.illustration_prompt: The devil with arms outstretched over a world of glittering kingdoms, demanding a bow. Jesus stands upright and unmoved. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto him, Get thee behind me, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered firmly: 'Get behind Me, Satan! It is written — You shall worship the Lord your God, and serve only Him!' Jesus said no with God's own words. The answer was clear, strong, and from the Bible.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Get away, Satan! Only God is to be worshipped!'
verses.meaning: Jesus quotes Deuteronomy 6:13. He names Satan directly, commands him away, and quotes Scripture. This is the model for standing firm against temptation.
verses.prayer: God, I choose to worship You and only You. Help me say no to anything that tries to take Your place.
verses.discussion_question: How do you think Jesus felt saying 'Get behind Me, Satan'? What gave Him the strength to say it?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus knew who to worship — and who not to. In a world full of competing loyalties, how do we keep God first?
verses.do_it_today: If something tempts you today, say out loud: 'No. I serve God only.'
verses.action_challenge: Write 'Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.' Put it somewhere you will see it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus pointing firmly away, face calm and resolute, while the devil retreats into shadow. A glowing Bible verse appears in the air between them. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he brought him to Jerusalem, and set him on a pinnacle of the temple, and said unto him, If thou be the Son of God, cast thyself down from hence:
verses.little_bible: The devil tried one more time. He took Jesus to Jerusalem — the holy city — and stood Him on the very top of the temple, the highest point. Then he said: 'If You really are the Son of God, jump off!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The devil put Jesus on top of the temple and said: 'Jump down!'
verses.meaning: The third temptation: use God's promises recklessly to test Him. Notice the devil now uses Scripture himself — but twists it. Even religious settings can be places of temptation.
verses.prayer: God, help me trust You rather than test You. I believe Your promises without needing to prove them.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it dangerous to try to use God's promises as a reason to do something reckless?
verses.family_discussion: The devil quoted Scripture here. How do we help our children recognise when the Bible is being used correctly versus being twisted?
verses.do_it_today: Think of a promise God has made to you. Trust it today — without demanding He prove it right now.
verses.action_challenge: Look up a picture of Jerusalem's temple. Imagine standing at the very top. What would it take to trust God up there?
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing on the highest point of the gleaming Jerusalem temple, high above the city, the devil beside Him. Jesus is perfectly still and unafraid. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: For it is written, He shall give his angels charge over thee, to keep thee:
verses.little_bible: The devil tried to use the Bible too! He quoted a psalm: 'It is written — God will send His angels to take care of You and keep You safe.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The devil said: 'The Bible says angels will keep You safe!'
verses.meaning: The devil quotes Psalm 91:11 — but he quotes it to justify a reckless act. He uses truth as a tool for disobedience. Scripture can be taken out of context and weaponised.
verses.memory_phrase: God's Word must be used rightly.
verses.prayer: God, teach me to read Your Word carefully so I use it the right way.
verses.discussion_question: Did the devil say something true? If so, why was what he was doing still wrong?
verses.family_discussion: The devil knows the Bible — he just uses it wrongly. How do we make sure we understand verses in their full context?
verses.do_it_today: When you read a Bible verse today, ask: what does the whole passage mean? Not just one line.
verses.action_challenge: Look up Psalm 91:11 and read verses 9-13. See how God's full promise of protection is meant for those who trust Him — not for foolish risks.
verses.illustration_prompt: An angel in shining armour with wings spread, hovering protectively — but the image is slightly distorted, seen through the lens of the devil's twisted use. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And in their hands they shall bear thee up, lest at any time thou dash thy foot against a stone.
verses.little_bible: The devil kept quoting the psalm: 'The angels will hold You up in their hands so You won't even hurt Your foot on a stone!' He made it sound completely safe — but he was twisting God's words.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The devil said: 'Angels will catch You so You won't get hurt!'
verses.meaning: The promise of God's protection is real — but it was never meant to justify throwing yourself off a building. Good promises misapplied become dangerous.
verses.memory_phrase: God's promises are not to test Him.
verses.prayer: God, I trust Your protection. I won't try to test it by doing something foolish.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between trusting God's protection and putting yourself in danger on purpose?
verses.family_discussion: There is a difference between faith and presumption. Faith trusts God in difficulty; presumption demands God prove Himself. How do you explain that to a child?
verses.do_it_today: Trust God's care today in a real situation — not by testing Him, but by quietly relying on Him.
verses.action_challenge: Draw an angel with open hands. Underneath write: 'God's protection is real — and it is not for testing.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Two gentle angels with open hands, ready to catch — but the image shows Jesus calmly refusing to jump, knowing this is not how trust works. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And Jesus answering said unto him, It is said, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered again with Scripture: 'It is written — you shall not put God to the test!' Even when the devil used the Bible, Jesus used the Bible back — correctly. Jesus always had the right answer.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus answered: 'It is written — do not put God to the test!'
verses.meaning: Jesus quotes Deuteronomy 6:16. For every twisted use of Scripture, there is a correct Scripture to answer it. Jesus knows the Word so well He can correct misuse instantly.
verses.memory_phrase: Do not test the Lord your God.
verses.prayer: God, help me know Your Word so well I can spot when it is being twisted.
verses.discussion_question: How did Jesus know the right verse to answer the devil's twisted use of the Bible?
verses.family_discussion: The best defence against a twisted Bible is knowing the full Bible well. What are you doing as a family to know Scripture more deeply?
verses.do_it_today: Memorise one verse this week. Even one verse stored in your heart is a weapon against lies.
verses.action_challenge: Write Luke 4:12 on a card and say it three times. Then write Deuteronomy 6:16 and say it too.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking calmly but with great authority, a Bible verse glowing in the air, the devil visibly shrinking back. Warm but powerful. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And when the devil had ended all the temptation, he departed from him for a season.
verses.little_bible: When the devil had tried every single test and failed every time, he left Jesus alone — for a while. He didn't give up forever, but for now, Jesus had won every battle.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The devil tried everything and failed. He went away — for a while.
verses.meaning: The devil left 'for a season' — meaning he would return. Victory over temptation is real but the battle is ongoing. Jesus won this round completely.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for winning against the devil. Help me stand firm when temptation comes back.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the devil left 'for a season' and not forever? What does that tell us about the battle?
verses.family_discussion: Temptation retreats but returns. How do we stay ready? How do we celebrate small victories without becoming careless?
verses.do_it_today: Celebrate one small victory over temptation today. Even saying no to a small wrong thing matters.
verses.action_challenge: Draw a scoreboard: Jesus 3, Devil 0. Remind yourself — Jesus wins every time.
verses.illustration_prompt: The devil's shadow retreating into darkness, Jesus standing in the wilderness bathed in warm light — victorious, peaceful, strong. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit into Galilee: and there went out a fame of him through all the region round about.
verses.little_bible: After His victory in the wilderness, Jesus went back to Galilee full of the Holy Spirit's power. And news about Him started spreading everywhere — all across the region, people were talking about Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus came back full of power. Everyone was talking about Him!
verses.meaning: The temptation was followed by power. Trials, when passed through with God, produce greater authority and reach. Jesus emerges stronger, and His ministry explodes.
verses.memory_phrase: After the test comes the blessing.
verses.prayer: God, help me trust that hard seasons make me stronger. Use my trials for Your glory.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus came back even more powerful after the hard time in the wilderness?
verses.family_discussion: Hard seasons can produce deeper character and greater anointing. Can you share a time when something difficult led to something fruitful?
verses.do_it_today: Think of something hard you are going through. Pray: 'God, use this to make me stronger for You.'
verses.action_challenge: Draw a map of Galilee. Imagine the news of Jesus spreading like ripples in water across every town.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus walking confidently into a bright, sunny Galilean town, a warm glow of the Spirit around Him, people turning to look with wide eyes. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he taught in their synagogues, being glorified of all.
verses.little_bible: Jesus went to the synagogues — the Jewish meeting places for worship and teaching — and He taught the people. And everyone praised Him and gave Him glory. His teaching was unlike anything they had heard.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus taught in the synagogues and everyone praised Him.
verses.meaning: Before any miracles are recorded in Galilee, Jesus teaches. Teaching — proclaiming God's Word — is the foundation of His ministry.
verses.prayer: God, help me love learning from Jesus. Let His words live in my heart.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think made Jesus's teaching so special that everyone praised Him?
verses.family_discussion: In an age of quick entertainment, how do we cultivate a love for sitting and learning from God's Word as a family?
verses.do_it_today: Sit quietly and read a few verses of Jesus's teaching today. What is He saying to you?
verses.action_challenge: Find a teaching of Jesus from the Gospels that you have never read before. Read it slowly.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing in a warm synagogue, people gathered around Him, listening with wonder and joy. Light streams through the windows. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up: and, as his custom was, he went into the synagogue on the sabbath day, and stood up for to read.
verses.little_bible: Jesus came to Nazareth — the town where He had grown up as a boy. On the Sabbath, as He always did, He went to the synagogue. It was His habit to go to worship. And He stood up to read from the Scriptures.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus went to His hometown and stood up to read in the synagogue.
verses.meaning: Jesus had a custom — regular, habitual worship. He went on the Sabbath as He always did. Faithfulness in worship is a practice, not just a feeling.
verses.prayer: God, help me make coming to You a habit — not just when I feel like it.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus went to worship regularly even when He was the Son of God?
verses.family_discussion: Regular habits of worship shape character over time. What worship habits is your family building?
verses.do_it_today: Do something worshipful today as a habit — not because it feels exciting, but because God is worth it.
verses.action_challenge: Make a plan for one new worship habit this week — Bible reading, prayer time, or worship music.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus entering a simple synagogue in Nazareth, familiar faces around Him, picking up a scroll to read. Warm, homely, reverent atmosphere. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And there was delivered unto him the book of the prophet Esaias. And when he had opened the book, he found the place where it was written,
verses.little_bible: They handed Jesus the scroll of the prophet Isaiah. Jesus unrolled it carefully and found the exact passage He was looking for — a passage that had been written hundreds of years before, waiting for this moment.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus unrolled Isaiah's scroll and found the right place.
verses.meaning: Jesus did not open at random — He found the exact place. This is intentional. What He is about to read is His mission statement, fulfilled in that very room.
verses.memory_phrase: Every word of Scripture has its moment.
verses.prayer: God, help me find the right words in Your Book for the right moment in my life.
verses.discussion_question: How do you think the people felt when Jesus opened the scroll and started to read?
verses.family_discussion: Isaiah wrote these words 700 years before Jesus. What does it mean for a prophecy to wait 700 years for its fulfilment?
verses.do_it_today: Open your Bible today and ask God to lead you to the right passage for your moment.
verses.action_challenge: Look up Isaiah 61:1-2. Read it knowing that Jesus was about to read it out loud in a synagogue in Nazareth.
verses.illustration_prompt: Hands carefully unrolling an ancient scroll, a soft golden light highlighting the text. The synagogue is hushed in anticipation. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he hath anointed me to preach the gospel to the poor; he hath sent me to heal the brokenhearted, to preach deliverance to the captives, and recovering of sight to the blind, to set at liberty them that are bruised,
verses.little_bible: Jesus read aloud: 'The Spirit of the Lord is on Me — He has anointed Me to bring good news to the poor, to heal the brokenhearted, to tell prisoners they are free, to give sight to the blind, and to set free those who are crushed.' This was Jesus telling everyone exactly why He had come.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus read: 'God sent Me to help the poor, heal broken hearts, and set people free!'
verses.meaning: Isaiah 61:1. This is Jesus's mission statement. He came for the poor, the broken, the captive, the blind, the bruised. The kingdom of God is for exactly the people the world overlooks.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus came for the broken and poor.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for coming for people just like me. Come and heal my heart too.
verses.discussion_question: Who are the poor, brokenhearted, and captive people Jesus came to help? Can you think of any around you?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus's ministry was to the overlooked. How is your family living out this mission — caring for the poor, the hurting, and the forgotten?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one person near you who is hurting or struggling. Do something kind for them today.
verses.action_challenge: Write out Isaiah 61:1 and highlight the five things Jesus came to do. Pin it up where your family can see it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus reading from the scroll with great purpose, the words glowing as He reads — images of the poor, the blind, the prisoner being set free float around the words. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: To preach the acceptable year of the Lord.
verses.little_bible: Jesus finished reading: 'To declare the year of the Lord's favour!' This was the Year of Jubilee — when debts are cancelled, slaves are freed, and everything is made new. Jesus was saying: that time is now.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus read: 'To tell everyone — now is God's time of favour!'
verses.meaning: The Year of Jubilee (Leviticus 25) was a year of complete restoration. Jesus declares He is the ultimate Jubilee — freedom, forgiveness, and new beginnings for all who come to Him.
verses.memory_phrase: Now is God's time of favour.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that now — today — is the time to come to You. I come to You right now.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think it means that Jesus declared 'the year of the Lord's favour'? What does God's favour feel like?
verses.family_discussion: Jubilee meant complete freedom and fresh starts. In what area does your family need to experience God's Jubilee — freedom from debt, fear, shame, or a broken pattern?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone today: 'Jesus brings freedom and fresh starts. It is not too late for you.'
verses.action_challenge: Look up Leviticus 25:10 — the original Year of Jubilee. See how Jesus fulfilled it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus lowering the scroll, arms wide open, light pouring out of the synagogue windows, as if the whole world is about to be set free. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he closed the book, and he gave it again to the minister, and sat down. And the eyes of all them that were in the synagogue were fastened on him.
verses.little_bible: Jesus rolled up the scroll and gave it back. Then He sat down — as teachers did in those days when they were about to teach. And every single person in the synagogue stared at Him. Nobody moved. Nobody spoke.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus sat down. Every eye in the room was fixed on Him.
verses.meaning: Sitting down was the position of a rabbi who was about to teach authoritatively. The silence and fixed gaze shows the room knew something extraordinary was happening.
verses.prayer: Jesus, fix my eyes on You today. I want to give You my full attention.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think every person was staring at Jesus? What do you imagine the room felt like?
verses.family_discussion: There are moments in worship when the room goes very still and everyone knows God is present. Has your family experienced a moment like that?
verses.do_it_today: Give Jesus your full attention for five minutes today — no distractions, just listening.
verses.action_challenge: Sit quietly for one full minute. No sounds, no phone, no movement. Just be still before God.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus sitting down in the synagogue, the scroll returned, every face in the room turned toward Him with wide, expectant eyes. Holy stillness. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he began to say unto them, This day is this scripture fulfilled in your ears.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus spoke. He said: 'Today — right now, as you listen — this Scripture has come true!' He was telling them plainly: I am the one Isaiah wrote about. I am the One God promised.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Today — this very day — this Scripture has come true!'
verses.meaning: This is one of the most dramatic statements in all of Scripture. Jesus claims to be the fulfilment of Isaiah 61 — the Anointed One, the Messiah — in the most direct way possible.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I believe You are who You say You are — the Messiah, God's Anointed One.
verses.discussion_question: How do you think the people felt hearing Jesus say 'This is fulfilled today'? What would you have thought?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus made an enormous claim here. Either He is telling the truth — or He is not. C.S. Lewis's 'liar, lunatic, or Lord' — discuss it together with your older children.
verses.do_it_today: Tell one person today: 'Jesus is who He said He is.' Say it with confidence.
verses.action_challenge: Find the prophecy in Isaiah 61:1-2 and compare it line by line with what Jesus did in the Gospels. Count how many were fulfilled.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking with quiet, absolute certainty in the synagogue, light flooding in, the scroll behind Him, every face leaning forward. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And all bare him witness, and wondered at the gracious words which proceeded out of his mouth. And they said, Is not this Joseph's son?
verses.little_bible: Everyone in the room agreed — His words were wonderful. They were amazed at how gracious and beautiful His teaching was. But then they started whispering: 'Wait… isn't this just Joseph's son? The carpenter's boy from here?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Everyone was amazed at His words. But they said: 'Isn't this just Joseph's son?'
verses.meaning: Familiarity breeds doubt. They were impressed but stumbled over His ordinariness. Knowing Jesus 'from before' became a barrier to receiving Him for who He truly is.
verses.prayer: God, help me never take Jesus for granted just because I've heard about Him many times.
verses.discussion_question: Why did knowing Jesus as 'Joseph's son' stop people from believing He was the Messiah?
verses.family_discussion: Can we become so familiar with Jesus — going to church, knowing the stories — that we stop being amazed by Him? How do we stay in wonder?
verses.do_it_today: Read one thing about Jesus today as if you are hearing it for the very first time.
verses.action_challenge: Write down three things about Jesus that still genuinely amaze you. Share them with someone.
verses.illustration_prompt: Faces in the synagogue switching from wonder to skepticism — furrowed brows, whispered words, a few people crossing their arms. Jesus remains calm. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Ye will surely say unto me this proverb, Physician, heal thyself: whatsoever we have heard done in Capernaum, do also here in thy country.
verses.little_bible: Jesus knew exactly what they were thinking. He said: 'You will tell Me the old saying — Doctor, cure yourself! You'll want Me to do here in My own hometown what you've heard I did in Capernaum.' He saw their expectation and their doubt at the same time.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'I know what you are thinking — prove yourself here too!'
verses.meaning: Jesus reads their hearts. They want a hometown performance — miracles on demand as proof. But faith is not built on demand miracles; it is trust, not spectacle.
verses.memory_phrase: Faith is not a demand for proof.
verses.prayer: God, I don't need You to prove Yourself to me. I choose to trust You.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it wrong to demand that God prove Himself before we believe?
verses.family_discussion: We often say in our hearts: 'God, if You do this, I will trust You.' How is that different from real faith?
verses.do_it_today: Is there something you are demanding God prove before you trust Him? Choose to trust Him today — without the proof first.
verses.action_challenge: Write: 'I choose to trust God even when I don't get a sign.' Put it somewhere you will see it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus with a knowing, gentle look, reading the crowd's skeptical faces. People in the background are folding their arms or raising eyebrows. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he said, Verily I say unto you, No prophet is accepted in his own country.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said plainly: 'The truth is — no prophet is ever welcomed in his own hometown.' People who know you from childhood sometimes find it hardest to believe God is truly at work in you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'No prophet is welcome in his own hometown.'
verses.meaning: A principle repeated across the Gospels. Familiarity and pride prevent recognition. The hometown crowd felt they 'knew' Jesus — and that knowledge became their barrier.
verses.prayer: God, open my eyes to see what You are doing — even in ordinary, familiar places.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think people from Jesus's hometown had a harder time believing in Him than strangers did?
verses.family_discussion: How do we receive God's work through people we already know — our spouse, our children, our old friends? Are we open to being surprised?
verses.do_it_today: Look for God at work in a familiar person today — someone you see all the time.
verses.action_challenge: Think of someone you know well. Ask God: 'What are You doing in their life that I might be missing?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking with calm certainty, a few older men of Nazareth in the background looking displeased or dismissive. Contrast of rejection and peace. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: But I tell you of a truth, many widows were in Israel in the days of Elias, when the heaven was shut up three years and six months, when great famine was throughout all the land;
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'Hear this truth — in the days of the prophet Elijah, there was a terrible drought that lasted three and a half years. No rain. No food. Many widows in Israel were starving.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'In Elijah's day, there was a long famine in Israel.'
verses.meaning: Jesus turns to Old Testament history — Elijah's drought (1 Kings 17-18). There were many in need in Israel, but God's miracle went to an outsider. This is the point Jesus is building to.
verses.memory_phrase: God's grace is never limited to one group.
verses.prayer: God, open my eyes to see how You love all people — not just people like me.
verses.discussion_question: What is a famine? Can you imagine what life would be like with no rain for three and a half years?
verses.family_discussion: God's provision in crisis: the famine lasted years. How do we trust God to provide during long, hard seasons — not just short ones?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for someone who is going through a long, hard season right now — not a short problem but a lasting trial.
verses.action_challenge: Read 1 Kings 17:1-7 — the beginning of Elijah's drought. See how God provided even in the most extreme circumstances.
verses.illustration_prompt: A dry, cracked landscape under a cloudless sky, a cluster of struggling people in the distance, and in the foreground a woman with empty hands. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: But unto none of them was Elias sent, save unto Sarepta, a city of Sidon, unto a woman that was a widow.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'But Elijah wasn't sent to any of those widows in Israel — God sent him to Zarephath, a city in Sidon, to a foreign widow!' God chose someone outside His own people to receive the miracle.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Elijah was sent not to Israel, but to a foreign widow in Sidon.
verses.meaning: Zarephath was Gentile territory. God sent His prophet to a foreigner, not to the people of Israel. Jesus is showing that God's grace cannot be owned or monopolised by any nation or group.
verses.prayer: God, help me never think Your love is only for people like me. You love the whole world.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think God sent Elijah to a foreign woman instead of one of the many widows in Israel?
verses.family_discussion: God's grace is not tribal or national. How are we as a family reflecting the wide, cross-cultural reach of God's love?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for someone from a different background, country, or culture than you.
verses.action_challenge: Read the story of the widow of Zarephath in 1 Kings 17:8-16. Notice what she did in faith and what God did in return.
verses.illustration_prompt: Elijah arriving at the gate of a foreign city, a widow woman there with her son, both looking thin and tired — but Elijah bringing hope. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And many lepers were in Israel in the time of Eliseus the prophet; and none of them was cleansed, saving Naaman the Syrian.
verses.little_bible: Jesus gave another example: 'In Elisha's day, there were many lepers in Israel who needed healing — but none of them were healed. The one who was healed was Naaman — a Syrian soldier, a foreigner!' God chose to bless an outsider.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: In Elisha's day, only Naaman the foreigner was healed of leprosy.
verses.meaning: Two examples, same message: God's grace goes beyond Israel's borders. Jesus is warning His hometown that assuming God's favour belongs exclusively to them is a deadly mistake.
verses.memory_phrase: God heals and blesses across all borders.
verses.prayer: God, I thank You that Your love has no borders. You love every person in every nation.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think Jesus is trying to show people with these two stories?
verses.family_discussion: Naaman was a Syrian soldier — an enemy. God healed him. Who are the people we might consider 'outsiders' that God deeply loves?
verses.do_it_today: Do something kind today for someone who is different from you — different background, age, or life situation.
verses.action_challenge: Read the story of Naaman in 2 Kings 5:1-14. How did pride nearly stop him from being healed? What changed his mind?
verses.illustration_prompt: Naaman the soldier standing in the Jordan River, being cleansed, surrounded by astonished onlookers. A foreign man receiving God's miracle. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And all they in the synagogue, when they heard these things, were filled with wrath,
verses.little_bible: When the people in the synagogue heard Jesus say these things, they were furious. They went from amazed to angry in an instant. The truth had cut too deep.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When Jesus said this, everyone in the synagogue became very angry.
verses.meaning: Truth that challenges privilege and assumptions produces anger. They had moved from wonder (v22) to fury. The gospel offends pride.
verses.memory_phrase: Truth can make us angry — or humble us.
verses.prayer: God, when Your truth challenges me, help me choose humility instead of anger.
verses.discussion_question: Why did what Jesus said make people so angry? What truth were they refusing to accept?
verses.family_discussion: We all have moments when something true makes us angry. How do we choose humility over defensiveness when we hear something we don't want to hear?
verses.do_it_today: If something true is hard to accept today, ask God to help you receive it humbly.
verses.action_challenge: Think of a time you got angry at something true. What happened in your heart? What would humility have looked like instead?
verses.illustration_prompt: The synagogue crowd shifting from wonder to fury — faces reddening, hands clenching, chairs scraping back. Jesus remains calm at the centre. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And rose up, and thrust him out of the city, and led him unto the brow of the hill whereon their city was built, that they might cast him down headlong.
verses.little_bible: The angry crowd jumped up, grabbed Jesus, and pushed Him out of the city. They dragged Him to the edge of the cliff on which the town was built — they were going to throw Him off! They wanted to kill Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The crowd grabbed Jesus and dragged Him to a cliff to throw Him off.
verses.meaning: The hometown crowd went from applause to attempted murder. Rejection of Jesus can be violent. But notice — they could not do what they intended. God's plan cannot be stopped.
verses.prayer: God, no plan of the enemy can stop Your purposes. I trust You.
verses.discussion_question: The crowd tried to kill Jesus but couldn't. What does that tell us about God's timing and protection?
verses.family_discussion: People often try to shut down the truth. But God's plans are unstoppable. Can you think of times in history or in your own life when someone tried to stop God's work and couldn't?
verses.do_it_today: When something feels like it is going to stop what God has planned for you, pray: 'God, Your plan cannot be stopped.'
verses.action_challenge: Look up Nazareth on a map. The traditional cliff is nearby. Imagine the scene and then imagine Jesus calmly walking through the crowd.
verses.illustration_prompt: An angry mob pushing Jesus toward a cliff edge above a steep drop, faces contorted with rage — but Jesus unmoved and serene in the middle of it. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: But he passing through the midst of them went his way.
verses.little_bible: But Jesus simply walked straight through the middle of the angry mob — and went on His way. They could not stop Him. He was not afraid. He just walked through them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus calmly walked right through the angry crowd and went on His way.
verses.meaning: One of the most powerful verses in Luke. No drama, no struggle — Jesus passes through as if the crowd is not even there. His hour had not yet come. Sovereign peace.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me walk through hard things with the same calm and peace You had.
verses.discussion_question: How do you think Jesus felt walking through that angry crowd? What gave Him such calm?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus did not run, argue, or fight. He simply walked through. What does that teach us about how to handle hostility or opposition with dignity?
verses.do_it_today: If you face a difficult situation today, walk through it calmly. You don't have to panic.
verses.action_challenge: Memorise Luke 4:30. It is a short verse with enormous power: 'But he passing through the midst of them went his way.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus walking serenely through the parting crowd, people falling back, unable to stop Him. Light surrounds Him. Peace in the middle of the storm. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And came down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee, and taught them on the sabbath days.
verses.little_bible: Jesus left Nazareth and went down to Capernaum, a city on the shore of Galilee. There He taught the people on the Sabbath — week after week, faithfully and powerfully.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus went to Capernaum and taught every Sabbath.
verses.meaning: Rejected at home, Jesus moves on to the place prepared for His ministry. Capernaum becomes His ministry base. When one door closes, God opens another.
verses.prayer: God, when I face rejection, help me not stay stuck. Help me move to where You are working.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus didn't give up teaching even after being rejected in Nazareth. What does that show us about perseverance?
verses.family_discussion: Rejection can derail us. How does your family handle rejection and move forward in what God has called you to?
verses.do_it_today: If you have been rejected or discouraged somewhere, ask God to show you where He is opening a new door.
verses.action_challenge: Find Capernaum on a map. This became the city of Jesus's miracles. God had a better place prepared after the rejection.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus arriving at the beautiful shore of the Sea of Galilee near Capernaum — the sparkling water, the welcoming town, a fresh start after rejection. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And they were astonished at his doctrine: for his word was with power.
verses.little_bible: The people in Capernaum were amazed at His teaching. There was something completely different about it — His words had real power. It was not like other teachers who just quoted other people. He spoke with God's own authority.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The people were amazed — Jesus taught with real power!
verses.meaning: Jesus's teaching had intrinsic authority — not borrowed from tradition or other rabbis. He spoke from the inside out. His word carried weight because He is the Word.
verses.prayer: Jesus, let Your powerful Word work in me today. Change me as I listen.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between someone who just talks about things and someone who really knows and lives what they are saying?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus spoke with authority because He was who He claimed to be. How does our personal relationship with God affect the weight of our words?
verses.do_it_today: Speak one truth about God today with full confidence — not as a quote, but as something you truly believe.
verses.action_challenge: Compare a verse from the Psalms with a teaching of Jesus in Matthew 5-7. Notice the difference in how each one speaks.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus teaching in Capernaum's synagogue, the crowd leaning in wide-eyed, His words seeming to glow with authority. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And in the synagogue there was a man, which had a spirit of an unclean devil, and cried out with a loud voice,
verses.little_bible: Right there in the middle of the synagogue — the holy worship place — there was a man with an evil spirit inside him. Suddenly, the evil spirit made the man cry out in a loud, frightening voice.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: In the synagogue, a man with an evil spirit cried out loudly.
verses.meaning: A demon in the synagogue. The forces of darkness can be present in religious spaces. They cannot stay silent in the presence of Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: Evil cannot stay silent before Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are more powerful than any darkness. Nothing evil can stand before You.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the evil spirit had to cry out when Jesus was near?
verses.family_discussion: Evil cannot stay comfortable in the presence of holiness. How does the presence of Jesus in our home and hearts affect the darkness around us?
verses.do_it_today: Invite Jesus into your space today — your room, your home, your heart. Darkness leaves when He arrives.
verses.action_challenge: Read Ephesians 6:10-12 about spiritual warfare. What does it mean to be strong in the Lord?
verses.illustration_prompt: A man in the middle of the synagogue congregation suddenly standing up, face contorted, crying out — the crowd startled around him. Jesus watches calmly. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: Saying, Let us alone; what have we to do with thee, thou Jesus of Nazareth? art thou come to destroy us? I know thee who thou art; the Holy One of God.
verses.little_bible: The evil spirit shouted through the man: 'Leave us alone, Jesus of Nazareth! What do You want with us? Have You come to destroy us? I know who You are — You are the Holy One of God!' Even the evil spirits knew exactly who Jesus was.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The evil spirit cried: 'I know who You are — the Holy One of God!'
verses.meaning: The demons had no doubt about Jesus's identity — the very thing the religious people in Nazareth questioned. Evil spirits feared and recognised Jesus perfectly.
verses.memory_phrase: Even demons know who Jesus is.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are the Holy One of God. I worship You with everything I have.
verses.discussion_question: Isn't it strange that the evil spirit knew Jesus better than many people did? What does that make you think?
verses.family_discussion: The demons believed Jesus existed — and trembled. James 2:19 says even the demons believe. Belief about Jesus is not the same as trusting in Jesus. Discuss the difference.
verses.do_it_today: Don't just believe about Jesus today — talk to Him. Trust Him. Relationship is more than knowledge.
verses.action_challenge: Write: 'Jesus is the Holy One of God.' Say it out loud as a declaration of faith — not just information.
verses.illustration_prompt: The demon speaking through the man, face twisted, pointing at Jesus — but Jesus unmoved and radiating holy authority. The contrast of fear and power. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, Hold thy peace, and come out of him. And when the devil had thrown him in the midst, he came out of him, and hurt him not.
verses.little_bible: Jesus spoke sharply to the evil spirit: 'Be quiet! Come out of him!' The evil spirit threw the man down hard — but then came out without hurting him at all. Jesus protected the man even as He freed him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Be quiet and come out!' The spirit left and did not hurt the man.
verses.meaning: Jesus's authority over evil is instant and complete. He commands with one sentence. The demon had no power to resist — and notice, Jesus protected the man even in the exit.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You have authority over everything. I am safe under Your protection.
verses.discussion_question: What are the two things Jesus said to the evil spirit? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus's two-word command: 'Come out.' Authority does not require long speeches. How do we model calm, godly authority with our children?
verses.do_it_today: When fear or a bad thought comes, say out loud: 'Jesus has authority over this.' Then pray.
verses.action_challenge: Find another time Jesus cast out a demon in the Gospels (e.g. Mark 1:21-28 or Mark 5:1-20). Compare what happened.
verses.illustration_prompt: The man falling to the ground, the dark spirit visible leaving him, Jesus standing with one hand raised in command — then the man sitting up, free and calm. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And they were all amazed, and spake among themselves, saying, What a word is this! for with authority and power he commandeth the unclean spirits, and they come out.
verses.little_bible: Everyone was stunned. They turned to each other asking: 'What kind of word is this?! He speaks with authority and power — He commands the evil spirits and they just obey!' Nobody had ever seen anything like this.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Everyone was amazed: 'He commands evil spirits and they obey!'
verses.meaning: Word and deed together — Jesus's teaching was powerful and so were His miracles. The people rightly concluded: this is something entirely new.
verses.prayer: Jesus, Your word is powerful. Speak into my life today and I will listen.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think the people felt when they saw this happen? What would you have said?
verses.family_discussion: The people were amazed at authority — not just words, but words that did things. How do we teach children that God's Word actually does things?
verses.do_it_today: Pick a promise in the Bible. Pray it today as a command into your situation: 'God, Your Word says ___. I believe it.'
verses.action_challenge: Write: 'Jesus commands and things happen.' Find one verse where God commands something and it comes to pass.
verses.illustration_prompt: The synagogue crowd buzzing with astonishment, hands pointing, eyes wide, people leaning to whisper to neighbours — all eyes still on Jesus. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And the fame of him went out into every place of the country round about.
verses.little_bible: And news about Jesus spread like wildfire — all through the region and into every surrounding area. Everyone was talking about the man from Nazareth who taught with power and freed people from evil spirits.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: News about Jesus spread everywhere in the region.
verses.meaning: The kingdom advancing. After each display of God's power, the report spreads further. Jesus's reputation was built on what He did, not what He claimed.
verses.prayer: God, let news of Your goodness spread through me — let people see You in my life.
verses.discussion_question: How do you think news spread so fast before phones and the internet?
verses.family_discussion: Word of mouth spread the story of Jesus. We still spread the story of Jesus by living it and telling it. How is your family doing that?
verses.do_it_today: Tell one person something Jesus has done in your life or family.
verses.action_challenge: Imagine being one of the people who saw Jesus free the man with the evil spirit. Write two sentences you would say to the first person you met outside.
verses.illustration_prompt: People rushing excitedly out of Capernaum along roads and paths, carrying the news, voices raised in wonder — the story of Jesus spreading like ripples. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he arose out of the synagogue, and entered into Simon's house. And Simon's wife's mother was taken with a great fever; and they besought him for her.
verses.little_bible: After the synagogue service, Jesus went to the house of Simon Peter. When He got there, Simon's mother-in-law was very sick with a high fever. The family came to Jesus and begged Him to help her.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus went to Simon's house. Simon's mother-in-law was very sick with a fever.
verses.meaning: From the synagogue to a private home. Jesus's ministry moved seamlessly from public to private. He cares about the sick in families just as much as in crowds.
verses.prayer: Jesus, come into my home and family. You are welcome here. Heal what needs healing.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the family asked Jesus to help instead of just treating the fever themselves?
verses.family_discussion: They brought their family problem to Jesus immediately. What does your family do when someone is sick or struggling — do you bring it to Jesus first?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for one person in your family or household right now. Ask Jesus to bring His healing and peace.
verses.action_challenge: Write a short prayer for each person in your family. Offer them to Jesus today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A simple warm home in Capernaum, a woman lying ill on a mat, family members gathered around, and Jesus entering the doorway bringing light with Him. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he stood over her, and rebuked the fever; and it left her: and immediately she arose and ministered unto them.
verses.little_bible: Jesus stood over her and spoke to the fever — He rebuked it, just like He rebuked the evil spirit. And the fever left immediately! She got up right away and began serving everyone in the room.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus rebuked the fever. She was healed immediately and got up to serve.
verses.meaning: Jesus heals with a word. The immediate recovery — she doesn't rest, she serves — shows the completeness of the miracle. True healing produces gratitude expressed in service.
verses.prayer: Jesus, heal me in every way — and help me use what You've given me to serve others.
verses.discussion_question: What did the woman do right after she was healed? What does that tell us about how we should respond to God's goodness?
verses.family_discussion: She got up immediately and served. When God heals or blesses us, the right response is to turn around and serve others. How does your family do this?
verses.do_it_today: Think of a way God has helped you recently. Do one act of service for someone else in response.
verses.action_challenge: Write down three things Jesus has done for you. Next to each one, write one way you can serve others because of what He has done.
verses.illustration_prompt: A woman getting up from a mat, fully well, immediately turning to serve a table of people — Jesus standing near with a quiet smile. Joy and gratitude in the room. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: Now when the sun was setting, all they that had any sick with divers diseases brought them unto him; and he laid his hands on every one of them, and healed them.
verses.little_bible: As the sun went down that evening, people came from all over — bringing their sick family members with all kinds of different diseases. And Jesus laid His hands on every single one of them and healed them all.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: At sunset, crowds brought their sick to Jesus. He healed every single one.
verses.meaning: The Sabbath ended at sunset, so people were free to travel and carry burdens. They came in multitudes, with every kind of sickness — and Jesus healed them all, one by one. He did not rush or overlook anyone.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You healed everyone who came to You. I come to You today with my need.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean that Jesus healed 'every one' — not some, but all? What does that tell you about Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus took time to lay hands on each person individually. In the middle of a crowd, He gave personal attention to each one. How do we make sure our family members each feel individually seen and cared for?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one person who is sick or hurting. Pray specifically for them today, using their name.
verses.action_challenge: Read this verse slowly three times. Count how many people Jesus healed: every one. Let that truth sink in deeply.
verses.illustration_prompt: The golden light of sunset over Capernaum, a long line of sick people waiting, Jesus moving from person to person, hands gently placed on each one. Warm, compassionate, unhurried. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And devils also came out of many, crying out, and saying, Thou art Christ the Son of God. And he rebuking them suffered them not to speak: for they knew that he was Christ.
verses.little_bible: Evil spirits came out of many people too, shouting loudly: 'You are the Christ, the Son of God!' But Jesus told them to be quiet and stopped them from speaking. He did not want demons announcing who He was.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Demons cried out: 'You are the Son of God!' Jesus told them to be quiet.
verses.meaning: Demons confessed the truth but Jesus silenced them. True testimony about Jesus must come from transformed lives, not from coerced declarations. The timing of revelation mattered.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus decides when and how His name is proclaimed.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I declare who You are not because I am forced to, but because I love You and believe.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus didn't want the demons announcing who He was, even though what they said was true?
verses.family_discussion: There is a difference between saying the right words and having a real relationship with Jesus. How do we help our children move from confession to connection?
verses.do_it_today: Tell Jesus who He is to you today — not because someone said you should, but from your own heart.
verses.action_challenge: Write a short declaration: 'Jesus, You are ___.' Fill it in with something true that you genuinely believe about Him.
verses.illustration_prompt: Evil spirits visible leaving people and crying out, Jesus with hand raised, silencing them with authority. Light and order being restored. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And when it was day, he departed and went into a desert place: and the people sought him, and came unto him, and stayed him, that he should not depart from them.
verses.little_bible: Early in the morning, Jesus quietly left and went to a lonely, quiet place to pray. But the crowds found Him! They came and tried to hold on to Him — they did not want Him to leave. They wanted to keep Him for themselves.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus went to pray alone. The crowd found Him and begged Him to stay.
verses.meaning: Even after the greatest ministry day imaginable, Jesus withdrew to pray. Solitude and prayer were not optional extras — they were the source of His strength. He also refused to be controlled by crowd pressure.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus always made time to pray.
verses.prayer: God, help me make quiet time with You a priority — even when life is busy and people want my attention.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus went away to pray even after healing so many people the night before?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus could have stayed with the crowd and done more miracles. He chose to pray instead. What does that tell us about the priority of prayer over activity?
verses.do_it_today: Find five quiet minutes today before you start your day or before bed. Just be with God.
verses.action_challenge: Mark a time in your calendar this week for just you and God — no agenda, just prayer and listening.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus alone in a quiet desert place at dawn, kneeling in prayer, a soft golden light around Him. In the distance, the crowd is coming. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, I must preach the kingdom of God to other cities also: for therefore am I sent.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said to the crowd: 'I must go and preach the good news of God's kingdom to other cities too — that is why I was sent.' Jesus had a mission bigger than one town. He would not stay just to make one group happy.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'I must tell the good news to other cities — that is why I was sent.'
verses.meaning: The word 'must' shows divine necessity — Jesus was on a mission, not a tour. The kingdom of God could not be contained in one city. Crowd pressure could not change His calling.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus came on a mission — and so do we.
verses.prayer: God, show me the mission You have sent me on. Help me not stay comfortable when You are calling me forward.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus said He must preach to other cities. What does the word 'must' tell you about how Jesus felt about His mission?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus refused to be contained by one group's preferences. How do we discern the difference between what people want from us and what God has actually called us to?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God: 'What have You sent me to do today?' Then listen — and go.
verses.action_challenge: Write your own 'I am sent to ___' sentence. What has God sent you into the world to do?
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus turning from the crowd gently but with purpose, His face set forward, pointing to the road ahead and the other cities in the distance. Mission in His eyes. Storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he preached in the synagogues of Galilee.
verses.little_bible: And so Jesus went and preached in all the synagogues across Galilee — town after town, Sabbath after Sabbath. He was faithful to the mission He had been sent to do.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus preached in all the synagogues across Galilee.
verses.meaning: The chapter ends quietly. No fanfare — just faithful, ongoing proclamation. Jesus continued in His mission: teaching, preaching, healing — faithfully, town by town.
verses.prayer: God, help me be faithful in the small, everyday things — not just the exciting moments.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to be faithful in doing something over and over again, even when it is not always exciting?
verses.family_discussion: Ministry and life are mostly built on faithful, quiet consistency — not just dramatic moments. How does your family celebrate quiet faithfulness?
verses.do_it_today: Do one faithful thing today that nobody might notice — serve quietly, pray quietly, give quietly.
verses.action_challenge: Think of one thing God has called you to do consistently. Write a plan to be faithful to it this week, even if it feels ordinary.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus walking a dusty road between towns, another synagogue visible ahead, set with purpose and faithfulness — the wide Galilean sky above. Storybook watercolor style.
